mechanism_narrative: A sustained multi-month decline in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals weakening corporate earnings expectations and consumer income growth, which lowers equity risk appetite and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following 20 trading days regardless of prevailing regime.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS 3-month change and forward 20d SPX returns stays below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or negative PAYEMS momentum periods show positive average forward returns with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When headline CPI (CPIAUCSL) year-over-year inflation exceeds 5%, markets price a higher probability of continued monetary restraint, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days as investors reprice discount rates.
failure_condition: In periods where CPIAUCSL YoY > 5%, SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the effect is not statistically distinguishable from unconditional base rate (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicate a broad-based contraction in aggregate demand spanning both durable and non-durable consumption, which jointly signal recession risk and drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days beyond what either series predicts alone.
failure_condition: Joint HOUST/RSAFS decline episodes fail to produce significantly more negative 60-day forward returns than either single-series signal alone (interaction term coefficient not significant at p < 0.05 in walk-forward regression).

mechanism_narrative: In a recessionary-onset regime marked by rising unemployment (UNRATE) alongside decelerating payrolls (PAYEMS), labor market deterioration signals impending earnings downgrades and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days; outside such regimes the same UNRATE uptick has muted predictive power.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp over 3 months while PAYEMS growth turns negative
failure_condition: During identified recessionary-onset windows, SPX 20-day forward returns are not significantly more negative than in expansion regimes (difference-in-means p > 0.05), or the signal fails to outperform a naive UNRATE-only baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent downtrend in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects contracting manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which anticipates falling corporate profits and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60 trading day horizon irrespective of other macro conditions.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO 6-month trend and forward 60d SPX returns remains below 0.03 across any 3-year rolling window, or downtrend periods do not show significantly lower average forward returns than uptrend periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) falls more than 25 basis points below its trailing 6-month average, markets interpret this as the onset of a monetary easing cycle that lowers the equity discount rate and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS drops > 0.25pp below its trailing 6-month average
failure_condition: Triggered easing episodes produce negative or flat average 60-day forward returns (mean <= 0) or the effect is not significant versus non-triggered periods at p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: In a housing-downturn regime where housing starts (HOUST) have fallen substantially from cyclical peaks, further deterioration in HOUST acts as a leading indicator of broader financial-sector stress and credit contraction, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days; in non-housing-stress regimes the same HOUST decline carries little predictive signal.
failure_condition: During housing downturn regimes, HOUST decline fails to predict negative 60d SPX returns with Rank IC < 0.03, or the effect size is statistically indistinguishable from HOUST behaviour in expansion regimes (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates below its prior 4-quarter trend pace, it signals an emerging output gap that pressures forward earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days once the deceleration is confirmed in the data.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth falls below prior 4-quarter average by > 1pp
failure_condition: GDPC1 deceleration triggers fail to produce significantly negative average 20-day forward returns (p > 0.05) or Rank IC stays below 0.03 across multiple GDP release vintages, indicating no robust signal despite revision risk.
